title: Ambient basic geometry
summary: Introduces the formal ambient bundle, its dilation and boundary inclusion, and the data used to represent a straight-form ambient metric.

mathematicalContent.heading: Ambient space and cone
mathematicalContent.body: Let G denote the conformal metric ray bundle over M. The Lean model adjoins a real coordinate ρ and calls the product the ambient bundle. The original ray bundle sits inside it as the hypersurface ρ = 0, and positive dilations act only in the ray direction.

mathematicalContent.heading: Straight-form data
mathematicalContent.body: A chosen scale s and a one-parameter family of concrete metrics g_ρ are stored, with compatibility at ρ = 0. This is the data needed for the familiar normal-form expression below; the file does not yet construct that pseudo-Riemannian tensor or its Levi-Civita connection.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The formal ambient total space is the product of the conformal metric ray bundle with one real coordinate ρ.
The concrete version retains an actual representative metric at each point rather than its conformal ray, and again adjoins ρ.
The ambient projection forgets both the ray representative and ρ, retaining the underlying base point.
On the concrete ambient bundle, the projection likewise returns the base point of the metric represented by u.
This is the second coordinate function on the formal ambient bundle.
This is the second coordinate function on the concrete ambient bundle.
The conformal metric ray bundle is included as the zero set of the ambient coordinate ρ.
The same zero-section inclusion is defined for the bundle of concrete pointwise metrics.
For r > 0, ambient dilation applies the conformal dilation δ_r to the ray variable and leaves ρ fixed.
The concrete dilation rescales the chosen metric representative by δ_r and does not change ρ.
This quotient map replaces a concrete pointwise metric by its conformal ray while preserving the ambient coordinate.
A scale s trivializes the metric ray bundle as ℝ₊ × M; adjoining the unchanged ρ-coordinate gives a trivialization of the formal ambient bundle.
correspondence.leanType: noncomputable def ambientTrivialization (s : Conf.Scale) : Conf.AmbientBundle ≃ ((PosReal × M) × ℝ)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Straight metric data consist of a base scale s, a real one-parameter family of concrete metric fields g_ρ, and the requirement that g₀ represents the conformal ray selected by s at every point.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \left(s,\{g_\rho\}_{\rho\in\mathbb R}\right),\qquad s\in\Gamma(\mathcal G),\quad g_\rho\in\operatorname{Met}(M),\quad [g_0(x)]=s(x)\;\forall x\in M.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For straight metric data A, g₀ is the member of the metric family at parameter ρ = 0.
This is the section of the conformal metric ray bundle determined by the base scale stored in A.
correspondence.leanType: def baseRaySection (A : Conf.StraightMetricData) : ∀ x : M, Conf.MetricFiber x

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A StraightForm is presently only a wrapper around StraightMetricData. It records the data conventionally used to write a straight ambient metric, but does not construct the tensor itself.
This accessor returns the base scale contained in a straight-form datum G.
This accessor returns the complete family ρ ↦ g_ρ contained in G.
The constructor regards a package A of straight metric data as a StraightForm without adding any further geometric information.

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, ambient space and normal form
references.note: Geometric source for the ambient bundle, dilation action, cone, and straight normal-form notation.

limitations: This module is a formal scaffold. It constructs product bundles, maps, and data structures, but StraightForm does not yet contain an actual ambient metric tensor, signature, smoothness assumptions, connection, curvature, or Ricci-flatness equation.
